title: Methods of production of fibrous fibrinogen scaffolds and products thereof
abstract: The present invention relates to methods for producing fibrous fibrinogen biomaterials that can be used as three-dimensional scaffolds. The methods of this invention enable the controlled detachment of fibrous fibrinogen scaffolds in vitro. The fibrous fibrinogen biomaterials generated by the methods of this invention can be detached in a solution. Alternatively, the fibrous fibrinogen scaffolds of this invention can be immobilized on a surface. The fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial can be used in medicine, such as in wound healing, regenerative medicine, dermal reconstruction, skin repair, bone vessel repair, blood vessel regeneration, tissue engineering, and implant coatings. The biomaterials can be generated "on-demand" and can be transferred to a site of injury, such as to a wound.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A method of generating a fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial, comprising a) Providing a substrate, b) Optionally, cleaning said substrate, c) Optionally, modifying of at least one surface of said substrate, d) Adding a fibrinogen solution to said substrate, or submerging said substrate in said fibrinogen solution, e) Adding a salt buffer and/or an aqueous buffer and/or water to said substrate, or submerging said substrate in said salt buffer and/or said aqueous buffer and/or said water, f) Optionally, drying said substrate, g) Optionally, adding a salt buffer and/or an aqueous buffer and/or water to said substrate, or submerging said substrate in said salt buffer and/or said aqueous buffer and/or said water, h) Optionally, drying said substrate, i) Optionally, performing one or more repetitions of steps g) to h), j) Fixating said substrate, and k) Washing said substrate, thereby generating said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial. 2. The method according to claim i, wherein said generated fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial is composed of three-dimensional fibrinogen fibers, optionally wherein said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial can be used as a scaffold 3. The method according to claim 2, wherein said fibrinogen fibers have a diameter of between 0.1 ran and 10000 nm, preferably between 1 nm and 1000 run, more preferably between 10 nm and 500 nm, and most preferably between 100 nm and 200 nm. 4. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial to be generated has a hydrodynamic radius of at least 0.00001 mm, preferably of at least 0.001 mm, more preferably of at least 0.01 mm, farther preferably of at least 0.1 mm, even more preferably of at least 1 mm, and most preferably of at least 10 mm. 5. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein said substrate as provided in a) is composed of a solid material, such as a solid glass material, optionally wherein said substrate comprises at least one surface composed of a metal or a polymer, preferably wherein said metal is selected from gold, silver, copper, gold-platted copper, tinned copper, aluminum, platinum, indium, tungsten, beryllium, gallium, lithium, calcium, magnesium, zinc, titanium, zirconium, hafnium, and mixtures thereof, more preferably wherein said metal is gold, or wherein said polymer is preferably selected from polylactide (PLA), polystyrol, polybutyrate adipate terephthalate (PBAT), and mixtures thereof, more preferably wherein said polymer is polylactide (PLA). 6. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein said modifying step c) is selected from silanization, esterification, phosphorization, hydrosilyation, physisorption, aldehyde modification, carboxylate modification, amine modification, epoxy modification, and mixtures thereof, preferably wherein said modification is silanization. 7. The method according to claim 6, wherein said silanization is performed by immersing the substrate in a silanization agent selected from 3- aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES), (3-aminopropyl)-dimethyl-ethoxysilane (APDMES), N-(2-aminoethyl)-3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane (AEAPS), 3- aldehydepropyltrimethoxysilane (APMS), mercaptopropyltrimethoxysilane (MPTMS), mercaptopropyltriethoxysilane (MPTES), biotin 4-nitrophenyl ester (BNPE), 11-hydroxyundecyl-phosphonate (HUP), organopolysiloxane, trimethylchlorosilane, and mixtures thereof, preferably wherein said silanization agent is 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane (APTES). 8. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein said fibrinogen solution as added in step d) has a fibrinogen concentration of between 0.01 and 1000 mg/ml, preferably between 0.1 and 100 mg/ml, more preferably between 0 5 and 50 mg/ml, and most preferably between 1 and 5 mg/ml. 9. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein said fibrinogen solution as added in step d) is an aqueous fibrinogen solution. 10. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein said salt buffer comprises at least one component selected from sodium phosphate, sodium chloride, acetic acid, ammonium carbonate, ammonium phosphate, boric acid, citric acid, lactic acid, phosphoric add, potassium chloride, potassium citrate, potassium metaphosphate, potassium phosphate (monobasic), sodium acetate, sodium citrate, sodium lactate, sodium phosphate (dibasic), sodium phosphate (monobasic), and mixtures thereof. 11. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein said salt buffer is phosphate-buffered-saline (PBS), optionally wherein said PBS comprises sodium chloride, potassium chloride, sodium phosphate, and potassium phosphate. 12. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 11, wherein fibrous fibrinogen nanofibers are generated when said optional steps g), h), and i) of said method are not performed, preferably wherein said buffer used in step e) is a salt buffer. 13. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein fibrous fibrinogen microfibers are generated when steps g), h), and i) of said method are performed, preferably wherein said buffer used in step e) and step g) is an aqueous buffer. 14. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein said fixating step j) is performed by immersing said substrate in a fixation agent selected from paraformaldehyde, formaldehyde, glutaraldehyde, mercury oxide, lead oxide, 

 osmium oxide, trichloroacetic acid, acetic acid, and mixtures thereof, preferably wherein said fixation agent is paraformaldehyde. 15. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein said washing step k) is performed in an aqueous solution. 16. The method according to any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein when said modifying step c) is performed prior to addition of the fibrinogen solution to the substrate in step d), and wherein said modifying is silanization of at least one surface of said substrate, said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial to be generated will be immobilized on said silanized surface of said substrate. 17. A fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial, produced by a method according to any of claims 1 to 16. 18. Use of a fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial in tissue engineering, wound healing, regenerative medicine, nerve regeneration, dermal reconstruction, sMn and/or bone vessel repair, blood vessel regeneration, implant coatings, biological filters, pharmaceutical screenings, pharmacological screenings, toxicological screenings, and/or biosensors. 19. A fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial for use as a medicament. 20. A fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial for use in the treatment of a degenerative disease, a wound, a dermal disease, a vessel damage, and/or a skin damage. 21. A method of treating a subject suffering from a degenerative disease, a wound, a dermal disease, a vessel damage, and/or a skin damage by administering to the 

 subject a fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial. 22. Use of a fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial for the manufacture of a medicament for use in the prevention and/or treatment of a degenerative disease, a wound, a dermal disease, a vessel damage, and/or a sMn damage. 23. A method for inhibiting bleeding at a target site in a patient's body, said method comprising delivering the fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial to a target site, a bleeding tissue, an abraded tissue surface, and/or a damaged tissue surface in an amount sufficient to inhibit said bleeding. 24. A method for delivering a bioactive substance to a target site in a patient's body, said method comprising delivering the fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17 or a composition comprising said fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial in combination with a bioactive substance to the target site. 25. The method according to claim 23 or 24, wherein the target site is a tissue selected from muscle, skin, epithelial tissue, connective tissue, supporting tissue, nerve tissue, ophthalmic and other sense organ tissue, vascular tissue, cardiac tissue, gastrointestinal organ tissue, pleura and other pulmonary tissue, kidney, endocrine glands, male and female reproductive organs, adipose tissue, liver, pancreas, lymph, cartilage, bone, oral tissue, mucosal tissue, spleen tissue, abdominal organ tissue, and combinations thereof. 26. The method according to any one of claims 23 to 25, wherein the target site is a bleeding site or bleeding tissue surface of a patient, or an abraded or damaged tissue surface of a patient. 27. The method according to any one of claims 23 to 27, wherein the target site is a void region within the selected tissue 28. The method according to claim 27, wherein the void region is selected from tissue divots, tissue tracts, intravertebral spaces, and/or body cavities. 29. The method according to any one of claims 23 to 28, wherein die tissue surface comprises an organ surface selected from the group consisting of a liver surface, a sMn surface, a spleen surface, a heart surface, a Mdney surface, an intestine surface, a blood vessel surface, a vascular organ surface, and a combination thereof. 30. A Mt comprising: a) a substrate, b) optionally, a modification agent, such as a silanization agent, c) a fibrinogen solution, d) a buffer; and e) optionally, a fixation agent. 31. A Mt comprising: a) a composition comprising a fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial according to claim 17, b) written instructions to apply the fibrous fibrinogen biomaterial onto a target site on a tissue; and optionally, a container holding the composition and the written instructions.
